title: Cozy Jamaican Potatoes Gratin
description: A rich, dairy-free, and flavorful Jamaican-inspired dish featuring tender potatoes in a creamy beurre-monte sauce.
instructions: In a large saucepan, combine the sliced potatoes, coconut milk, jerk seasoning, garlic powder, onion powder, salt, and black pepper.
Cook over medium heat, stirring occasionally, until the potatoes are slightly tender, about 10 minutes.
In a separate saucepan, melt the coconut oil over medium heat.
Add the flour and whisk to combine; cook for 1-2 minutes, or until lightly golden.
Slowly pour in the coconut milk, whisking constantly to prevent lumps.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook for 2-3 minutes, or until thickened.
Stir in the scallions and cilantro.
Transfer the potato mixture to a 9x13-inch baking dish.
Top with the beurre-monte sauce and smooth the top.
Bake for 25-30 minutes, or until the potatoes are tender and the top is golden brown.
Serve warm, garnished with additional scallions and cilantro if desired.
